summary.intent: Add a large library of default bundled skills to Moltis (ported from Hermes Agent and OpenClaw) so the agent has rich capabilities out of the box, with a web UI to browse them by category.
summary.outcome: 101 bundled skills embedded in the binary via include_dir!, with category metadata, web UI category filter + search + inline detail view, auto-install hints for skills with missing binaries, and all system prompt/API endpoints updated to include bundled skills. PR #797 created, reviewed by Greptile twice, and all comments resolved.

repo: Skills are discovered from 4 sources (Project > Personal > Registry > Plugin); bundled was added as lowest priority (5th source).

The system prompt skills block is built in crates/chat/src/prompt.rs (discover_skills_if_enabled), NOT in the gateway — this was a critical missing wire that caused bundled skills to not appear in agent sessions.
The web API skill listing (/api/skills) in crates/web/src/api.rs creates its own FsSkillDiscoverer independently from the gateway, so it needed separate wiring.
skill_tools.rs was later split into a skill_tools/ module on main; merge conflict required re-applying changes to read_ops.rs.

The biome formatter (used in CI via local-validate.sh) requires more aggressive line-breaking in arrow functions than prettier: body statements inside JSX event handlers need one extra tab level of indentation.
check-file-size.sh applies a 1500-line limit to all files; .moltis/ (user-installed skills) and bundled skill sidecar files need explicit exclusions.
YAML unquoted values cannot start with @ — npm scoped package names like @steipete/summarize must be quoted in SKILL.md frontmatter.
Auto-install runs on the gateway host process, but exec tool runs inside the sandbox container — these are different environments, so auto-install was changed to return install instructions instead.

code.finding: BundledSkillStore uses include_dir! for release builds and filesystem reads for dev mode (same pattern as crates/web/src/assets.rs). Discovery is recursive to handle 3-level paths like mlops/training/axolotl/SKILL.md.
CompositeSkillDiscoverer merges FS + bundled discovery with name deduplication using a HashSet; FS skills override bundled by name.
discover_skills_if_enabled() was using plain FsSkillDiscoverer — the critical missing wire. Must use CompositeSkillDiscoverer for bundled skills to appear in agent system prompts.

Three-tier prompt format fallback: Full (name+desc+attrs, default 30KB budget) → Compact (self-closing tags, no desc) → Truncated (binary search for largest prefix). Clippy lint: use div_ceil() instead of manual (lo+hi+1)/2.

ReadSkillTool::execute must treat empty string file_path the same as null/absent to avoid model retry loops. Auto-install logic moved to return install_note instructions rather than running installs on the gateway host.
skill_detail RPC handler needed a Bundled branch — without it, clicking bundled skills in web UI did nothing. Added skill_detail_bundled() function.

Category filter pills and search input added above the skills table. Detail panel rendered inline as a full-width <tr> after the active row (not below the table). Bundled skills hide the Disable button.

workflow: When copying vendored skill files from other repos, always scan SKILL.md frontmatter YAML for special characters (@, :, etc.) that need quoting.
Directory name must match the frontmatter 'name' field exactly for read_skill lookups to work — mismatches cause silent failures discoverable only via tests.
The origin: metadata block in SKILL.md serves as provenance tracking for vetted bundled skills.
Feature flags must be forwarded through the full crate chain (skills → tools → gateway → web → cli) for include_dir! embedded assets to compile correctly.
Greptile automated code review caught real issues: path traversal in bundled sidecar reads, intra-set deduplication gap, CDN SRI hash missing, and bundled red-teaming skill included by default.
local-validate.sh runs biome for TS formatting with stricter rules than prettier — arrow function bodies in JSX props need explicit multi-line formatting.

friction: Biome formatter required multiple iterations to fix indentation — the error output shows character-level diffs with arrows that are hard to parse, and running biome --write locally didn't produce the expected output due to version differences.

The critical wire in crates/chat/src/prompt.rs was missed in initial implementation — bundled skills appeared in web UI and via read_skill but not in agent system prompts, causing the agent to be unaware of them.
Auto-install ran on gateway host instead of sandbox — npm scoped package install failed with EINVALIDPACKAGENAME, wasting multiple debugging cycles before identifying the environment mismatch.
npm --ignore-scripts flag broke scoped package installation (@steipete/summarize), requiring removal.
skill_tools.rs was refactored into a module on main between implementation and merge, requiring re-application of all changes to the new file structure.
Background task notifications required separate reads to get results, and some tasks appeared to complete before being pushed.
YAML @ character issue in requires: frontmatter caused silent parse failures for 5 skills, caught only by running tests.
open_items: The 'plan' skill still references '.hermes/plans/' directory — should be updated to use Moltis workspace conventions.
The 'google-workspace' skill references Hermes OAuth2 setup patterns — needs Moltis-specific OAuth flow documentation.

Ineligible skills (missing required binaries) appear in the system prompt with no indication they're non-functional — annotating them as eligible=false in the prompt would help the agent skip them proactively.
Some bundled skills still have lingering Hermes/OpenClaw references in body text that weren't caught by the bulk cleanup regex (e.g. OpenClaw-specific tool names in messaging skills).
obliteratus skill (LLM refusal removal) is bundled by default — security review of whether this is appropriate for a default-enabled skill.
The youtube-content skill and summarize skill both handle YouTube summaries but with different approaches — they could be consolidated or cross-referenced.
Auto-install was changed to return instructions rather than run installs — a future improvement could detect whether the exec tool is available and route installs through it automatically.
